channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Watch Duty overtakes ChatGPT in the App Store as California wildfires spread
item.description: As devastating fires continue to rage in the Southern California area, an app that tracks wildfires with live maps and alerts has become the No. 1 free app in Apple’s App Store.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: The Italian Government's Deal with SpaceX: A Matter of National Interest or Private Gain?
item.description: Italy's Premier Giorgia Meloni has sparked controversy over a potential deal with Elon Musk's SpaceX, worth 1.5 billion euros. The project would provide encryption services for the Italian government and communications infrastructure for the military and emergency services.
item.dc:creator: GIADA ZAMPANO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 4 in 10 companies planning job cuts due to AI: Survey
item.description: Companies across the globe, at least 4 in 10, are likely to cut jobs as artificial intelligence (AI) continues to advance, new research shows.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Beware of confusing naming strategies with JLab's new headphones, but the perks look good
item.description: JLab's new over-ears deliver ANC, wireless sharing and lots of features for typically low prices –and also confusing naming strategies.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Nvidia RTX 5080 benchmark suggests this will be a mighty laptop GPU, getting PC gamers excited about the RTX 5090 mobile
item.description: If this purported benchmark is on the money, we could be looking at some seriously peppy RTX 5000 mobile graphics cards.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Lawmakers request GAO review of TSA’s biometrics, facial recognition use
item.description: TSA has expanded its use of facial recognition technology to more than 80 airports across the country in recent years. The post Lawmakers request GAO review of TSA’s biometrics, facial recognition use first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Musk emerges as Trump foreign policy wild card
item.description: Elon Musk has increasingly waded into foreign politics from the perch of his social platform X — a development that experts say likely raises questions for U.S. allies about his role in foreign policy, and stir frustration within the incoming Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Sarakshi Rai, The Hill

item.title: Google faces privacy lawsuit as judge highlights data governance concerns
item.description: A federal judge in San Francisco has ruled that a privacy lawsuit against Google, alleging the company improperly collected personal data from mobile devices, can proceed.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Creature Commandos season 1 ending explained: who dies, is there a post-credits scene, will there be a season 2, and more of your biggest questions answered
item.description: Creature Commandos' season 1 finale is out now – here's what happens in it and whether it sets up future DCU projects.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: AWS to invest $11 billion in Georgia to expand infra for gen AI
item.description: Amazon Web Services (AWS) is planning to invest $11 billion in the state of Georgia to expand its infrastructure in the US to support the growing demand for generative AI, AI, and other cloud technologies.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Meta tries letting Facebook Marketplace users view eBay listings as way to resolve EU charges
item.description: Meta said Wednesday that it will allow some Facebook users to view eBay listings on its Marketplace service, as it tries out a possible way to resolve European Union charges of anticompetitive behavior that the bloc leveled last year.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Meta rolls back hate speech rules as Zuckerberg cites 'recent elections' as a catalyst
item.description: It wasn't just fact-checking that Meta scrapped from its platforms as it prepares for the second Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Flying taxis could be carrying passengers in 2025, but hurdles remain
item.description: When he was still a boy making long, tedious trips between his school and his woodsy home in the mountains during the 1980s, JoeBen Bevirt began fantasizing about flying cars that could whisk him to his destination in a matter of minutes.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: From AI assistants to holographic displays, automakers showcase in-cabin experiences at CES
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Interior lighting aimed at reducing motion sickness. Shiny holographic dashboards displayed on windshields. And AI-powered voice assistants paired with infotainment systems to keep you company on long drives.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 2025 could be the year of the robot lawn mower
item.description: Skeptics scoffed in 2002 when Roomba introduced the robot vacuum to homeowners. That didn’t last very long as the little round device quickly became a fixture in millions of households.
item.dc:creator: Chris Morris, Fast Company

item.title: New research shows a quarter of freshwater animals are threatened with extinction
item.description: New research shows about a quarter of animals living in rivers, lakes and other freshwater sources are threatened with extinction.
item.dc:creator: Christina Larson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Japan links Chinese hacker MirrorFace to dozens of cyberattacks targeting security and tech data
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japan on Wednesday linked more than 200 cyberattacks over the past five years targeting the country's national security and high technology data to a Chinese hacking group, MirrorFace, detailing their tactics and calling on government agencies and businesses to reinforce preventive meas
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Top tech stealing the show at CES 2025
item.description: Big tech coming in 2025 includes solar umbrellas, AI TVs, smart earbuds and crazy robots. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson gives his take on the wonders revealed at CES 2025.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Meta Changes Content Moderation Policies Amid Trump Threats
item.description: Mark Zuckerberg's Meta has announced changes to its content moderation policies on Facebook and Instagram, a move widely seen as an attempt to appease the incoming Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, BARBARA ORTUTAY and NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Silicon Valley homeless nonprofit sues Microsoft over disrupted email access
item.description: The nonprofit LifeMoves claims the disruption has made it difficult to connect homeless people with services.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Varian, The Mercury News

item.title: Meta's fact-checking changes put company under political microscope
item.description: Meta’s decision to eliminate its fact-checking program marks a sharp reversal for the major social media company, quickly prompting questions over the firm’s direction as President-elect Trump heads back to the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: US probing Teslas over driverless feature
item.description: The U.S. government is probing Tesla over a driverless feature in its electric vehicles, according to the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA).
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang says he’d be delighted to meet and congratulate Donald Trump
item.description: As head of the world’s most valuable chipmaker, Huang has an opportunity to help steer the administration’s AI policy at a moment of rapid change
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Lost your contacts on your phone? Here's how to get them back
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson provides two methods to restore your contacts for iPhone and Android devices.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 5 ways data teams must lead in AI-driven organizations
item.description: Generative AI has inspired a surge of interest in using data to improve the accuracy of business decisions.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: With o3 having reached AGI, OpenAI turns its sights toward superintelligence
item.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman has reinvigorated discussion of artificial general intelligence (AGI), boldly claiming that his company’s newest model has reached that milestone.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Mark Zuckerberg denies claims of building doomsday bunker in Hawaii
item.description: "I think it got blown out of proportion as if the whole ranch was some kind of doomsday bunker," Zuckerberg said.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: OPM CIO Cavallo retiring after 23 years in government
item.description: Guy Cavallo has been the Office of Personnel Management’s CIO since 2020 and also worked at SBA, TSA and began his career working for the Senate. The post OPM CIO Cavallo retiring after 23 years in government first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Fubo combining with Disney's Hulu + Live TV; lawsuit against Venu Sports settled
item.description: Disney's Hulu + Live TV and sports streaming service Fubo are combining in a deal that will also see the settlement of a lawsuit against the creation of Venu Sports.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: WordPress.org statement threatens possible shutdown for all of 2025
item.description: Editor’s note: On Jan. 3, 2025, WordPress.org staff announced that they were resuming the suspended services. Automattic CEO Matt Mullenweg on Friday announced a shutdown of almost all services on WordPress.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: AI revolution drives demand for specialized chips, reshaping global markets
item.description: Artificial Intelligence (AI) has rapidly transformed the chip industry since its mainstream arrival over the past two years, driving demand for specialized processors, accelerating design innovation, and reshaping global supply chains and markets.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: China protests US sanctions for its alleged role in hacking, complains of foreign hacker attacks
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — China has slammed a decision by the U.S. Treasury to sanction a Beijing-based cybersecurity company for its alleged role in multiple hacking incidents targeting critical U.S. infrastructure, while the Chinese cyber security agency complained Monday of attacks on Chinese networks.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: What to expect from NaaS in 2025
item.description: Enterprises have been slow to adopt NaaS for a number of reasons, including lack of clarity on exactly what NaaS is, lack of a solid business case that NaaS saves money over the long term, and questions around security, visibility, control, and data privacy.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Someone needs to make AI easy
item.description: 2024 was a year filled with AI possibilities; 2025 needs to turn those possibilities into realities. For that, we need the cloud companies to stop churning out “primitives” such as large language models (LLMs) and instead give developers solutions.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Create custom visuals on your iPhone with Image Playground in iOS 18.2
item.description: Apple's Image Playground transforms text prompts into images or animations in seconds, brining a world of creativity to the palm of your hand.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NASA's Next-Generation Mars Helicopter Set to Revolutionize Red Planet Exploration
item.description: NASA's new Mars helicopter design promises to be a significant upgrade from Ingenuity, featuring six rotors and the ability to carry 11-pound science payloads.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Police took over a week to name the NYC subway burning victim. A fake name spread in the meantime
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — It took police more than a week to publicly identify Debrina Kawam, 57, as the woman who was fatally set on fire in a New York subway train last month. But on the internet, it took just hours for a false name to begin spreading.
item.dc:creator: MELISSA GOLDIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Elon Musk stays on brand following Tesla Cybertruck explosion
item.description: Elon Musk used his X account to defend the durability of Tesla's Cybertruck after the New Year's Las Vegas explosion.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Indiana bill could force kids under 16 off of social media without parental permission
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS--- If State Sen. Mike Bohacek’s (R-Michiana Shores) bill passes next session, social media platforms would need to block kids under 16 from accessing their content if they don’t receive their parents’ permission to use their sites. ”All we’re trying to do is protect kids,” Bohacek said.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Adamson, NewsNation
